free_text: The elastic scattering of deuterons by carbon.
The  mean energy of the incident beam was known to
about +-10 keV and the energy spread was about 5 keV.
The 2800 ohm-cm n-type surface barrier
detectors were used.
4.5*4.5 mm solid state detector.
The data were converted from a relative scale to an
absolute scale. First, an excitation function was
measured at the laboratory angle 167.3 degr. over the
energy range 3.0 to 3.4 MeV and was normalized to the
absolute cross section data of McEllistrem et al.
Then, without moving the target foil, several points
were obtained at each energy at which an angular
distribution had been measured. For these points,
laboratory angles in the backward hemisphere were
chosen where the cross sections varies slowly with
angle. An error of about +- 15% may be assigned to the
normalisation.
The target consisted of a self-supporting carbon foil
about 50 mu-g/cm2 thick was used. The distance from
the target to the detector was 5.5 cm.
Dead time corrections were never allowed to exceed 6%.
Statistical error
Data digitizing error
Normalisation error
Energy digitizing error
Error from the graphic bars
Fig.2
Angle digitizing error
In some cases angular errors near 90 degr. as large as
2 degr. were caused by the change in the foil position.
These errors have been corrected by using pulse-height
together with an accurate (1%) energy calibration of
the detector to indirectly determine the scattering
angle.
